meta_title: Impact Doors in Margate, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Margate, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your home — free quote today!
h1: Margate's Trusted Choice for Impact Doors That Stand Up to South Florida Storms
intro:
Margate, Florida sits in the heart of Broward County — a community of hardworking families, well-established neighborhoods, and homes that have weathered some of the most powerful storms in Atlantic hurricane history. From the tree-lined streets of Oriole Estates and Coral Gate to the tightly-knit blocks of Margate Estates and South Margate, homeowners here understand that protecting a property worth an average of $310,000 means more than just hoping for the best when a storm rolls in. Impact doors are one of the smartest, most cost-effective upgrades any Margate homeowner can make — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is here to make that upgrade seamless, affordable, and built to last.

Unlike basic storm doors or accordion shutters that require last-minute scrambling before a hurricane, impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors provide 24/7 passive protection with no preparation required. Margate sits approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, which means it sits squarely in a 170 mph wind zone. That wind load is nothing to take lightly. When a major hurricane tracks up the peninsula or cuts inland from the coast — as history has shown they absolutely can — the pressure differentials and wind-driven debris that reach Margate's residential streets can be catastrophic to standard doors. Impact doors rated for Broward County's Florida Building Code requirements are engineered specifically to handle those forces without failing.

Approximately 50% of Margate's housing stock was built before 1994 — meaning a significant portion of homes were constructed before modern hurricane protection standards were introduced in the wake of Hurricane Andrew. Many of those homes still have original entry doors, aging sliding glass doors, and hollow-core French doors that offer little resistance to a serious storm. Upgrading to code-compliant impact doors isn't just about safety — it's about preserving the investment you've made in your Margate home, lowering your homeowner's insurance premiums, and gaining peace of mind every time a tropical system enters the Gulf or Atlantic. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped more than 500 South Florida families make that upgrade, and we're ready to help yours.

section_why.h2: Why Margate Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home, you deserve a team that treats your property with the same care you do. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — known throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as "The Kings of Service" — has built its reputation on exactly that standard. With over 60 years of combined installation experience and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, we're not a fly-by-night operation chasing storm-season work. We're a committed, licensed, and insured South Florida contractor with deep roots in the communities we serve, including right here in Margate.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We don't subcontract your project to a third-party crew with unknown training or accountability standards. Every impact door we install — whether it's a single entry door on a South Margate ranch home or a full set of sliding glass doors facing the backyard in Coral Gate — is handled by our own trained, experienced technicians. This means the quality control never leaves our hands, and our warranties carry real weight. We know the product, we know the code requirements, and we know how to install correctly the first time.

Families in Oriole Estates and Margate Estates are often working with established homes that require thoughtful, experienced hands during any door replacement project. Older door frames may need reinforcement, sill conditions may require attention, and rough opening dimensions can vary widely in pre-1994 construction. Our crews are trained to address those real-world conditions rather than simply swapping hardware and walking away. We pull all required permits through the City of Margate Building Division, coordinate inspections, and ensure every installation meets or exceeds current Florida Building Code requirements — so you never have to worry about compliance issues down the road.

Financing is also a cornerstone of our service model, because we believe impact door protection shouldn't be reserved only for higher-income households. With financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available, Margate homeowners across all income brackets can access the same premium products and expert installation that protects their families and their investments. We work with you to find a payment structure that fits your budget, so there's no reason to delay the protection your home deserves.

section_history.h2: Margate's Storm History Makes Impact Door Protection a Non-Negotiable Investment
section_history.body:
Since 1990, Margate and the surrounding Broward County region have been impacted by no fewer than 9 named tropical systems — a sobering statistic that reflects just how vulnerable inland South Florida communities truly are. The most defining of these events was Hurricane Andrew in August 1992, a Category 5 monster that crossed South Florida and demonstrated in the most devastating way possible what poorly constructed or unprotected homes cannot survive. Although Andrew's eye tracked through Miami-Dade County, wind fields extended well into Broward, and the building code overhaul that followed fundamentally changed what "storm-ready" meant for every home built or renovated afterward.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered a direct and forceful reminder that Broward County is not immune. Wilma made landfall near Cape Romano and crossed the state at near-Category 3 intensity, striking the Fort Lauderdale metro area with sustained winds that left widespread damage from Tamarac to Margate and beyond. Entry doors blew in, sliding glass doors shattered, and homes that lacked impact-rated products suffered significant interior damage from wind and rain intrusion. For many Margate families, Wilma was the moment they realized storm shutters were not enough — and that permanently installed impact protection was the only truly reliable solution.

Hurricane Irma in September 2017 reinforced that message again. Though Irma made Florida landfall near Marco Island, its enormous wind field impacted virtually all of South Florida, with Broward County experiencing tropical storm to Category 1 equivalent conditions for an extended period. Neighbors in Coconut Creek, Coral Springs, North Lauderdale, and Tamarac all saw similar effects. Homes with impact doors and windows fared dramatically better than those relying on traditional shutters or, worse, no protection at all. That pattern of repeat exposure is precisely why impact door upgrades in Margate aren't a luxury — they're a logical, data-backed investment in the long-term safety of your home and family.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Margate and Broward County
section_code.body:
Margate falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), and while it is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — it still carries stringent wind-resistance requirements that all new and replacement impact doors must meet. The current FBC requires that impact doors installed in Margate's 170 mph wind zone be tested and certified to withstand large missile impact testing, cyclic pressure loading, and structural performance standards consistent with the wind speeds the area is designed to handle. These standards are not optional — they are enforced through the permitting process and verified during inspection.

All impact door replacements in Margate require a permit pulled through the City of Margate Building Division before any work begins. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the entire permit process on your behalf — from submitting the application and product approval documentation to scheduling and passing all required inspections. This protects you as the homeowner from liability and ensures that your installation is fully documented for insurance purposes, future resale, and compliance with your mortgage lender's requirements. Never let a contractor suggest skipping the permit process — unpermitted impact door installations can create serious complications if you ever file a storm damage claim or attempt to sell your home.

Product approval is a critical component of Broward County compliance. Every impact door we install carries a current Florida Product Approval (FPA) number, verifying that it has been independently tested to meet the applicable FBC standards for your specific wind zone. Our ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI door products carry the necessary certifications for Margate installations, and our team is fully versed in the approval documentation requirements the City of Margate Building Division expects. From initial design selection through final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the compliance process completely — because getting it right the first time saves everyone time, money, and stress.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Built for Broward County's Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two industry-respected brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce hurricane-impact-rated door systems that are specifically engineered for South Florida's demanding conditions — combining structural integrity, energy efficiency, and aesthetic appeal in products that are built to perform for decades. For Margate homeowners looking to upgrade entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors, these brands offer a comprehensive range of styles, sizes, and configurations to suit virtually any home design.

ESW impact entry doors are constructed with reinforced frames, heavy-duty multi-point locking systems, and impact-laminated glass panels or solid impact-rated panel construction depending on the design selected. Multi-point locking is a particularly important feature for Margate's older housing stock — where a single-point deadbolt on a pre-1994 door frame offers minimal resistance to wind pressure — because it distributes the locking load across the full height of the door, dramatically increasing resistance to both forced entry and storm pressures. ESW's sliding glass door systems are equally impressive, featuring reinforced stile and rail construction, marine-grade hardware, and interlocking meeting rails that seal tightly under positive and negative wind loads. Their French door configurations are available in a variety of grille and glass patterns that complement the architectural styles found throughout Oriole Estates and Coral Gate.

CWI impact doors offer Margate homeowners an excellent alternative, particularly for projects where specific sizing, custom configurations, or budget considerations are a priority. CWI products are manufactured with the same Florida Product Approval requirements in mind, and their door systems have earned strong reviews among South Florida contractors and homeowners for their clean finishing quality and long-term hardware durability. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door or upgrading every exterior door opening in your home, our team will help you select the right ESW or CWI product for your specific needs — ensuring a perfect fit, code compliance, and outstanding performance for years to come.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Margate — and How Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Makes It Affordable
section_cost.body:
For Margate homeowners with a median home value around $310,000, the question of impact door cost is a reasonable and important one. The truth is that pricing varies based on the type of door — entry, sliding glass, or French — as well as the size, configuration, glass type, and frame finish you select. A single impact-rated entry door installation in Margate typically ranges from approximately $1,500 to $3,500 fully installed, depending on product selection and any frame preparation required. Sliding glass door replacements generally range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more for larger openings. French door systems fall in a similar range. For homeowners upgrading multiple openings at once, package pricing can significantly reduce the per-unit cost.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these investments accessible through flexible financing options that require no money down. With monthly payments starting as low as $87, a Margate homeowner can begin protecting their property immediately without waiting to accumulate the full purchase price. Our financing programs are designed with lower-middle-income households in mind — because the families in South Margate and Margate Estates who have worked hard to own their homes deserve the same level of protection as anyone else. We'll walk you through all available options during your free in-home consultation so you can make the right financial decision for your family.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors consistently deliver. Broward County homeowners who install impact-rated doors and windows routinely see meaningful reductions in their annual homeowner's insurance premiums — sometimes hundreds of dollars per year — through wind mitigation credits. Those savings compound over time, effectively reducing the real cost of your installation every year. Impact doors also add measurable value to your home at resale: buyers in Margate's competitive market recognize the value of impact protection, and real estate professionals consistently report that fully impact-protected homes sell faster and at stronger prices than comparable unprotected properties.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors in Margate Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowner's insurance has become one of the most significant household expenses for Broward County residents, and Margate families are feeling that pressure acutely. The good news is that installing impact-rated doors — particularly when combined with impact windows — can generate substantial insurance savings that make your protection upgrade even more financially attractive. Under Florida law, insurance carriers are required to offer premium discounts or credits to policyholders who have wind mitigation features in place, and impact-rated openings are among the most impactful qualifying factors.

The wind mitigation inspection process is the formal mechanism through which your savings are calculated. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and permitted, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can visit your home and document the impact-rated doors and windows, opening protection ratings, and roof-to-wall connection details. That report is submitted to your insurance carrier, who then applies the applicable credits to your premium. For Margate homeowners with older homes — particularly those built before 1994 — the difference between a pre-upgrade and post-upgrade wind mitigation report can be dramatic, with some policyholders reporting annual savings of $500 to $1,500 or more depending on their insurer and coverage level.

Common carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Heritage, Universal Property & Casualty, and others — all have wind mitigation credit structures in their rating systems. If you're currently with Citizens, Florida's insurer of last resort, maximizing your wind mitigation credits can also help you qualify for or maintain your coverage eligibility as the state continues to push policyholders toward the private market. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is happy to provide the documentation and product approval numbers your wind mitigation inspector will need to complete their report accurately and quickly. Protecting your home and reducing your insurance costs at the same time is one of the clearest win-win investments available to Margate homeowners today.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if I'm 8 miles from the ocean in Margate?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Distance from the coast doesn't eliminate hurricane risk in South Florida. Margate sits in a 170 mph wind zone, and storms like Hurricane Wilma (2005) and Hurricane Irma (2017) demonstrated that inland Broward County communities experience destructive wind speeds, flying debris, and pressure-driven door failures just as coastal areas do. Impact doors provide essential year-round protection regardless of how far inland you live.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Margate, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact entry door installations in Margate typically range from $1,500 to $3,500 per door fully installed, while sliding glass and French door systems range from $2,500 to $6,000 or more depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available, making it possible to begin protecting your home without a large upfront investment. We'll provide a detailed, no-obligation quote during your free consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Margate?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door replacements in Margate require a permit from the City of Margate Building Division, along with Florida Product Approval documentation for the specific products being installed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application submission to final inspection — ensuring your installation is fully compliant with Florida Building Code requirements and properly documented for insurance and resale purposes. Never skip the permit process on an impact door project.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Margate home?
faqs.a: The typical impact door installation project in Margate takes one to two days for the physical installation work, depending on the number of openings and any frame preparation required. Lead times from contract signing to installation date vary by season and product availability, but generally range from two to six weeks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the permitting timeline concurrently with product ordering to minimize total project duration. We'll give you a realistic schedule estimate during your consultation.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases. Florida insurance carriers are required to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with impact-rated opening protection. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and inspected, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your improvements and submit a report to your carrier. Margate homeowners — especially those in older pre-1994 homes — can often see annual premium reductions ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars depending on their insurer, coverage amount, and the full scope of their impact protection upgrades.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation consultation — our expert team will assess your home, walk you through ESW and CWI door options, and help you find a financing plan that works for your budget. The Kings of Service are ready to serve Margate.
